_meta.purpose: U2 diagnostic — verify BQ dual-tc row shape for smoke build

_finding_summary: BQ app_automate_test_sessions_partitioned returns ONE ROW PER SESSION, regardless of how many testcases the session contains. The test_status field is a JSON-stringified counts breakdown (passed/failed/timedout/skipped/error/running/queued) — per-tc COUNTS only, NOT per-tc durations. The duration field is session-level total (1153 s for the smoke). No separate per-testcase table exists in the app_automate dataset (verified via INFORMATION_SCHEMA.TABLES LIKE '%testcase%' / '%maestro%').
_implication_for_u4: Per-tc duration breakdown must come from the local BS Maestro v2 build JSON (results/<run-id>/<build-id>.json, nested devices[].sessions[].testcases[] structure), not BQ. The report's session-level percentile tables (matching iOS 'Cloud — phase percentiles (BigQuery)' section) can use BQ; the dual-yaml-mechanics section's per-tc duration distribution must use the build JSON.
